Clone source-effect contract: each normal clone_from call maps valid pre-source and pre-destination owners to valid post-source and post-destination owners under the joint declared source and destination behavior-effect relations. Both shells remain valid; surviving leaves keep roots, overwritten or otherwise ended leaves end once, every new leaf is relation-authorized, and a unique transfer ends at its source before destination liveness and is never live in both. Destination storage reuse grants no root. Repeated calls use the preceding post-source and post-destination states.

input_ownership: Shared borrows. The Hash member branch receives a caller-owned mutable Hasher H, uses only call-scoped reborrows for writes, and neither moves nor destroys H. Only HashMap/HashSet equality iterates the left operand and probes the right through get/contains, so only right-hand S is invoked; left-hand S remains retained and unreborrowed. Length mismatch and empty equality perform zero build_hasher calls, while each performed right-hand probe creates exactly one generated H. During such a probe the same right-hand S owner remains valid, but S leaves may end, be replaced, or move into H only under the BuildHasher behavior-effect and result relations; a transferred unique leaf ends in S before becoming live in H and is never live in both. Generated H evolves only under the Hasher behavior-effect relation and is destroyed exactly once after its probe. Separately, only the Hash implementation branch uses caller-owned H and never invokes BuildHasher: the same H owner returns to the caller, while its leaves may end, be replaced, or move only under the Hasher behavior-effect relation. Other comparison branches use neither role. Across every equality, ordering, and hashing branch, shared operand shells remain valid on normal return but either invoked operand's internal leaf map may evolve only under the selected behavior-effect relation; shared access implies neither purity nor an unchanged operand leaf map.
post_state_result: Relation/hash result
invalidation: None Surviving S/H leaves keep exact roots and new leaves follow their declared behavior/result relations. No leaf derives from a temporary reborrow, &S, S-field storage, or the call frame; a transferred leaf retains only its independent external root. Neither stored S, generated H, nor caller-owned H mints relation-result, payload, occupancy, uniqueness, equivalence, or check-elision authority. The stored-S/generated-H equality path and caller-H Hash path must never be unioned into every comparison.
failure_drop_abandonment: Behavior inconsistency is contained logic error for keyed structures, never memory unsafety. Every normal relation/hash return leaves H in the caller with its updated state and exact capture provenance. Length mismatch and empty equality perform zero BuildHasher calls; every performed right-hand get/contains probe owns and destroys exactly one generated H. Caller-owned H is never destroyed by Hash.
